content_body: Located within the Chattahoochee-Oconee National Forests, the Brasstown Bald Visitor Information Center is the highest point in Georgia, rising 4,784 feet above sea level and just a short drive from the GA towns of Blairsville, Hiawassee, and Helen. On the Bald, there are picnic areas with great views, a general store with locally made products, three trailheads just off the parking lot, a mountaintop natural science & history museum, and an observation deck that offers a spectacular 360°, including four states (GA, TN, NC, & SC) and if you’re lucky, the skyline of Atlanta on clear days!
title: Brasstown Bald
